fix.generatedCode: src/scriptSecurityAnalyzer.js } - The count of lines containing the 'sudo' command */ async function analyzeSudoUsageInScripts(searchDir, filePrefix) { if (!searchDir || typeof searchDir !== 'string') { throw new Error('searchDir must be a non-empty string'); } if (!filePrefix || typeof filePrefix !== 'string') { throw new Error('filePrefix must be a non-empty string'); } // Use find to get all .sh files, then filter in JS to avoid command injection // This is safer than constructing shell commands with user input const escapedDir = searchDir.replace(/'/g, "'\\''"); const command = `find '${escapedDir}' -type f -name "*.sh"`; try { const { stdout } = await execAsync(command, { maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 * 10 }); const files = stdout.split('\n').filter(f => f.trim()); let totalCount = 0; for (const filePath of files) { // Only count files that start with the given prefix const fileName = path.basename(filePath); if (fileName.startsWith(filePrefix)) { try { const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'); const lines = content.split('\n'); for (const line of lines) { if (line.includes('sudo')) { totalCount++; } } } catch (e) { // Skip files that can't be read } } } return totalCount; } catch (error) { // When no matching files are found, the command may still succeed but with empty output if (error.message && error.message.includes('No such file or directory')) { return 0; } if (error.code === 'ENOENT') { return 0; } throw error; } } module.exports = { analyzeSudoUsageInScripts }; ]]>
